name: Riverbank of the Cabrón stream — circular from Figueruela de Arriba
name_original: Ribera del arroyo Cabrón — circular desde Figueruela de Arriba
region_intro: Hiking around Figueruela De Arriba offers access to varied landscapes, including parts of the Montesinho Natural Park and the Sierra de la Culebra. The region features river valleys, such as the Rio de Onor, alongside rolling hills and forested areas. The terrain provides a mix of easy paths and more challenging moderate ascents, with 7 hiking trails available. Best hiking trails around Figueruela De Arriba The most popular hiking route is The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11) — circular route in the Montesinho Natural Park, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 52 minutes to complete, offering views of the river and natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil), a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This route leads through natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Local hikers also love the Barranco Cibeo y Peña Mira — circular por la Sierra de la Culebra, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Culebra, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Figueruela De Arriba is defined by river valleys, natural park trails, and the hills of the Sierra de la Culebra. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy hiking trails and moderate routes. The routes in Figueruela De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Figueruela De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Figueruela De Arriba?
A: There are 7 hiking trails around Figueruela De Arriba, offering a range of experiences from easy riverside paths to moderate ascents in the hills.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy trail perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424/the-portuguese-side-of-rio-de-onor-pr-11-circular-route-in-the-montesinho-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) circular route that takes about 1 hour 52 minutes to complete, offering pleasant views of the river and Montesinho Natural Park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: The region offers diverse landscapes, including parts of the Montesinho Natural Park and the Sierra de la Culebra. You'll encounter picturesque river valleys like the Rio de Onor, rolling hills, and forested areas, providing a mix of scenery.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424/the-portuguese-side-of-rio-de-onor-pr-11-circular-route-in-the-montesinho-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11)&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025160778/barranco-cibeo-y-pena-mira-circular-por-la-sierra-de-la-culebra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Cibeo y Peña Mira&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or landmarks to see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover various points of interest. Along the Rio Onor, you might find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fidionda Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mira Summit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers great views.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes around Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, easy trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424/the-portuguese-side-of-rio-de-onor-pr-11-circular-route-in-the-montesinho-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11)&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 2 hours, while moderate routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022480114/route-of-the-wolves-return-from-trabazos-de-aliste" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Wolves&lt;/a&gt; might take over 4 hours.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the Montesinho Natural Park, and the well-maintained paths that offer a mix of challenges and scenic views.

Q: Are there any specific trails known for wildlife spotting?
A: The region, particularly the Sierra de la Culebra, is known for its wildlife. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2167070666/deer-trail-pr12-guadramil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Trail (PR12 Guadramil)&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, passes through natural landscapes where you might have a chance to spot local fauna, including deer, which the trail is named after.

Q: Can I find historical or cultural sites while hiking in the area?
A: Yes, the region has some cultural points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio de Onor Village&lt;/a&gt; is a unique settlement straddling the border, and you might also encounter an &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7173693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Stone Church&lt;/a&gt; along some routes, offering a glimpse into the local heritage.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Montesinho Natural Park?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2172563424/the-portuguese-side-of-rio-de-onor-pr-11-circular-route-in-the-montesinho-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Portuguese Side of Rio de Onor (PR 11)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically located within the Montesinho Natural Park, providing direct access to its landscapes and views, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1956537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of São Julião&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the difficulty level of most hikes in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: While there is one easy trail, the majority of the 7 available routes are classified as moderate. These moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025243526/pena-beneita-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Beneita Route&lt;/a&gt;, typically involve more varied terrain and some elevation changes, suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.
tips: Not far from the town of Figueruela de Arriba, we find magnificent views of the landscape as we walk along this wide dirt road.
At this point, near the Cabrón stream, it is worth stopping for a few seconds to observe the views that this fabulous environment offers us.
Beautiful and picturesque wooden and stone bridge that allows us to cross the Cabrón stream.
